drafts.title: AboutMeDraft interface - writer is routing-agnostic
drafts.compiledTruth: writeAbrainAboutMe accepts an AboutMeDraft interface where region (identity|skills|habits), routingConfidence [0,1], routeCandidates string[], and routingReason are pre-decided inputs passed in by the router. The writer itself does NOT participate in routing decisions; it only echoes these fields into frontmatter for audit and doctor tooling. This clean separation keeps the writer as a pure storage layer. Additional fields: title (<=80 chars), body (>=20 chars), optional triggerPhrases, tags, status (default active), slug override, timelineNote, sessionId. WriteAboutMeOptions carries abrainHome, settings, optional dryRun and auditContext.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. Lane G writer 函数

drafts.title: identity/skills/habits live at abrain-home top-level, not under projects/
drafts.compiledTruth: Lane G entries land at ~/.abrain/<region>/<slug>.md where region is identity, skills, or habits. These three directories are direct children of abrainHome, NOT nested under projects/. writeProjectEntry's projectId binding, project-scoped paths, and <projectRoot>/.pi-astack/ infrastructure are explicitly not reused. Rationale: Lane G writes cross-project truths about the user, so project-binding would be semantically wrong and would break cross-project memory_search retrieval.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. Lane G writer 函数

drafts.title: about-me.lock is separate from workflow.lock to prevent cross-lane blocking
drafts.compiledTruth: The Lane G writer uses <abrainHome>/.state/sediment/locks/about-me.lock, independent from workflow.lock used by Lane B1 (writeAbrainWorkflow). Both lock files share the same locks/ directory but have independent lifecycles. This prevents a stalled Lane G write from blocking Lane B1 and vice versa. The lock separation mirrors the logical independence of about-me zone writes from workflow zone writes.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. Lane G writer 函数

drafts.title: Lane G writer reuses the full writeAbrainWorkflow substrate
drafts.compiledTruth: writeAbrainAboutMe reuses all helpers from the writeAbrainWorkflow (B1) substrate without forking: sanitizeForMemory (applied to title/body/trigger/tags/timelineNote), lintMarkdown, abrain-side lock, audit row to abrainSedimentAuditPath with lane:about_me (pre-reserved in ADR 0014 audit enum), git commit to abrain repo, atomic write via tmp+rename, git rollback on failure (unlink + git reset HEAD if gitCommit is null). This avoids a separate substrate and keeps all abrain writers aligned on the same failure/recovery mechanics.
drafts.sourceHeading: D1. Lane G writer 函数

drafts.title: MEMORY-ABOUT-ME fence + /about-me slash are symmetric dual triggers
drafts.compiledTruth: Lane G has two entry points that converge on the same extractor-router-writer chain: (1) MEMORY-ABOUT-ME: fence in any user message, parsed by parseExplicitAboutMeBlocks (structurally identical to parseExplicitMemoryBlocks with only the fence header changed; end marker is still END_MEMORY; fence-in-code-block skip rule is identical to Lane A). (2) /about-me <text> slash command, which wraps the text into a MEMORY-ABOUT-ME block injected into the transcript. Both paths converge at the same parser. This is symmetric to Lane A (MEMORY: fence) and Lane C (auto-extractor).

drafts.title: /about-me slash injects fence into transcript; never calls writer directly
drafts.compiledTruth: The /about-me slash handler does NOT call writeAbrainAboutMe directly. It appends a MEMORY-ABOUT-ME block to the transcript as a user-role message via pi.sendUserMessage. Sediment's agent_end picks this up in the next LLM turn. This preserves ADR 0014 invariant #1 layer 1: main session TUI command handlers cannot hold direct brain mutation entry points. Each /about-me triggers one LLM turn (assistant typically replies Noted), accepted as the cost of maintaining the layer-1 mechanic. If pi SDK does not support user-role inject, fallback is printing a fence template for the user to paste; invariant is maintained either way.

drafts.title: Lane G router: lane allowlist, LLM classifier, staging gate (3 stages)
drafts.compiledTruth: about-me-router.ts implements ADR 0014 §3.5 three-stage router: Stage 1 (deterministic lane allowlist): laneAllowlist[about_me] = {identity, skills, habits, staging}; validateRouteDecision immediately rejects any target outside this set with route_rejected audit. Stage 2 (LLM aboutness classifier): reuses curator model + JSON-mode + temperature=0; returns chosen_region, route_candidates, routing_reason, routing_confidence. Stage 3 (staging gate): routing_confidence < 0.6 routes to staging path instead of identity/skills/habits. All three stages are mandatory; validateRouteDecision is the final enforcer. Missing fields, lane-target mismatch, or unmet confidence gate all throw RouterError; writer writes nothing except a route_rejected audit row plus original input to staging/rejected/.

drafts.title: Lane G can never route to knowledge/, workflows/, or projects/
drafts.compiledTruth: Lane G's laneAllowlist[about_me] is strictly {identity, skills, habits, staging}. validateRouteDecision (Lane G hard exclusions) treats any routing target of knowledge/, workflows/, projects/, or vault/ as a hard gate violation, emits route_rejected audit and throws RouterError. No fallback, no soft failure. The writer writes zero markdown and zero audit rows on a gate violation (only the route_rejected row plus input to staging/rejected/ for sample preservation). This is a physical firewall against cross-lane contamination; the Lane G classifier is also explicitly configured to refuse vault/ entries (vault has its own Lane V entry via /secret).

drafts.title: Classifier confidence < 0.6 routes to staging, never directly to identity/skills/habits
drafts.compiledTruth: When the LLM aboutness classifier returns routing_confidence < 0.6, the router routes the entry to projects/<active>/observations/staging/<date>--<pid>--<sessionStartEpoch>.md. The staging entry's frontmatter carries route_candidates and routing_reason for subsequent review-staging tooling. Staging entries are excluded from memory_search. Promotion to identity/skills/habits requires explicit review-staging action (deferred to G4). This enforces ADR 0014 §3.5 trust budget: uncertain classification does not directly pollute about-me zones.

drafts.title: Semantic boundaries for identity/skills/habits used in classifier system prompt
drafts.compiledTruth: The LLM aboutness classifier system prompt encodes these judgment hints (per ADR 0014 §3.5): identity/: stable self-descriptions, values, cognitive preferences, personal narrative (e.g. 'I am allergic to over-engineering'). habits/: observed repeated behavioral patterns, statistical tendencies (e.g. 'I tend to run smoke tests before committing'). skills/: technical or meta-capability inventory (e.g. 'I know TypeScript'). The identity-habits boundary is inherently soft: 'I value type safety' (identity, stable value) vs 'I tend to enable strict mode' (habits, behavioral tendency) can describe the same reality. The classifier gives soft-boundary cases low confidence, triggering the staging gate.

drafts.title: frontmatter region field is source of truth - not inferred from directory path
drafts.compiledTruth: Every Lane G entry has region: identity|skills|habits|staging in its YAML frontmatter. This is the canonical source of truth. memory_search and review-staging tooling read region from frontmatter, NOT by reverse-engineering the file's directory path. This prevents desync if entries are physically moved (e.g. staging to identity promotion). The physical location and frontmatter region must match, but frontmatter wins if they diverge. This also future-proofs against any directory restructuring without requiring re-inference logic.

drafts.title: skills/habits confidence decay deferred from P0 (YAGNI - no data yet)
drafts.compiledTruth: ADR 0014 §2.2/§2.3 specified 90-day reinforcement decay for skills/habits (confidence decay to status:deprecated). This is NOT implemented in P0. writer defaults confidence=5 with no auto-decay schedule. Rationale: YAGNI; no real entry accumulation yet, so no empirical basis for calibrating a decay curve. The confidence frontmatter field is reserved and passes ENTRY validation but is static. Decision should be revisited once the zones accumulate meaningful entry counts. Decay curve design depends on observed signal half-life, which cannot be estimated in advance.

drafts.title: Lane G slug must be globally unique across all four dirs
drafts.compiledTruth: Before writing any Lane G entry, the writer scans all four directories (identity/, skills/, habits/, staging/) for slug collisions. If the intended slug already exists in any of the four, the write is rejected and a dedupe_collision audit row is emitted. Cross-region slug uniqueness is required so staging entries can be promoted to identity/skills/habits without slug conflicts. Current implementation is O(N) full scan, acceptable at near-zero entry count; an index is deferred until entry count exceeds ~1000.

drafts.title: MEMORY-ABOUT-ME fence has same trust surface as Lane A; sub-agent exfiltration is known residual
drafts.compiledTruth: MEMORY-ABOUT-ME: fence inherits Lane A's trust boundary via two mechanical defenses: (a) parseExplicitAboutMeBlocks isInsideCodeFence uses the same code-block skip rule as parseExplicitMemoryBlocks; (b) sediment in dispatch sub-agent children performs ephemeral_session early-return in agent_end, so sub-agents do not trigger Lane G writes. However, sub-agent output re-enters the main session transcript via tool_result, so the main session's sediment will encounter any MEMORY-ABOUT-ME blocks that sub-agents wrote. This is the same residual surface as Lane A per ADR 0014 trade-off #9 (LLM prompt injection). Lane G adds no additional mechanical defense.

drafts.title: identity/skills/habits included in world store walker from G1 without config change
drafts.compiledTruth: After G1 shipped, memory_search's world store walker automatically covers identity/, skills/, and habits/ because the world store root is abrainHome and the walker only explicitly excludes projects/ and vault/. The three Lane G top-level dirs fall through as included. No change to resolveStores was needed (correcting an earlier P1-7 audit concern). What remains for G5 is region-aware ranking: using frontmatter.region as a boost signal in RRF fusion, distinct from the 2.0x project store and 0.3x other-projects weights.
drafts.walkback: 2026-07-21 implementation-alignment note: compiledTruth is preserved as the historical ingest record. It is not a live retrieval contract: current stage0 uses ordered dense, freshness reserve, sparse, and stale candidate assembly with bounded deduplication, not score fusion.

drafts.title: Staging dirs excluded from memory_search via relpath-anchored ignore, not basename match
drafts.compiledTruth: Staging directories (observations/staging and observations/staging/rejected) are excluded from memory_search via STAGING_IGNORE_REL_PATHS in parser.ts scanStore using relpath-anchored matching. Basename matching is explicitly rejected: it would incorrectly exclude any directory named staging anywhere in abrain. listFilesWithRg uses --glob exclusions for observations/staging and observations/staging/rejected as the primary path. The world store already excludes projects/ via WORLD_EXTRA_IGNORE_DIRS, so staging (which lives under projects/) is indirectly excluded there; the explicit STAGING_IGNORE_REL_PATHS guard is on the project store scanner.

drafts.title: identity-habits boundary is soft in NL; single-shot LLM classification may be unstable
drafts.compiledTruth: The semantic boundary between identity/ and habits/ is inherently fuzzy: 'I value type safety' (identity, stable value declaration) vs 'I tend to enable strict mode' (habits, behavioral tendency) can describe the same underlying reality with different framings. A single LLM classifier call at temperature=0 may produce inconsistent region assignments for edge cases, especially if the user's phrasing shifts between sessions. Mitigation: confidence < 0.6 routes to staging instead of polluting either zone; frontmatter carries routing_reason and route_candidates for audit; review-staging allows user correction. The smell is managed but not eliminated.

drafts.title: routingReason is a canonical fixed string, not derived from timelineNote
drafts.compiledTruth: In G1/G2 (pre-LLM-classifier phases), routingReason is always the canonical fixed string 'user-attested via MEMORY-ABOUT-ME fence (G1)'. It must NOT be sourced from or conflated with timelineNote. Distinction: routing_reason is routing rationale (why the router chose this region, consumed by validateRouteDecision audit and doctor tooling); timelineNote is narrative context about the entry's timeline (Timeline section in the entry body). A G2 bug was caught where these were conflated, causing a router rejection reason to appear as the routing rationale. When G3 LLM classifier is enabled, the LLM's output rationale will replace this fixed string.

drafts.title: agent_end checkpoint advances only when BOTH Lane A and Lane G succeed
drafts.compiledTruth: When Lane A (MEMORY: fence) and Lane G (MEMORY-ABOUT-ME: fence) are both active in the same agent_end cycle, combinedShouldAdvance = laneA_shouldAdvance AND laneG_shouldAdvance. Neither lane advances the session checkpoint if the other fails. Each audit row carries checkpoint_advanced:combined and lane_advance_decision:<self> fields for forensic tracing. Rationale: if Lane A succeeds but Lane G's git commit fails, advancing the checkpoint would cause the failed Lane G write to silently lose its retry opportunity in the next session. The mutex ensures both lanes get a retry window on partial failure.

drafts.title: ENTRY_KINDS natural mapping per Lane G region: identity to maxim/preference, skills to fact, habits to pattern
drafts.compiledTruth: Lane G uses the existing ENTRY_KINDS enum without adding new values. Convention: identity/ entries map to maxim or preference (stable values, cognitive preferences, personal narrative); skills/ entries map to fact (technical or meta-capability inventory); habits/ entries map to pattern (observed recurring behavioral tendencies). The region frontmatter field (not kind) is the definitive discriminator for which zone an entry belongs to. This mapping is a classifier prompt convention, not a hard constraint enforced by the writer or validator. An identity entry with kind:fact is technically valid; kind is secondary metadata.
